Replication article reference: Ziano, I., Mok, P. Y. (Cora), & Feldman, G. (2020). Replication and Extension of Alicke (1985) Better-Than-Average Effect for Desirable and Controllable Traits. Social Psychological and Personality Science, 12(6), 1005---1017. https://doi.org/10.1177/1948550620948973
Outcome: "successful"
Outcome quote: We successfully replicated the trait desirability effect, such that participants rated more desirable traits as being more descriptive of themselves than of others (original: [Formula: see text] = .78, 95% confidence interval [CI] [.73, .81]; replication: sr 2 = .54, 95% CI [.43, .65]). The effect of desirability was stronger for more controllable traits (effect of Desirability -- Controllability interaction on self---other-ratings difference; original: [Formula: see text] = .21, 95% CI [.12, .28]; replication: sr 2 = .07, 95% CI [.02, .12]). [...] Thirty-five years later, the better-than-average effect appears to remain robust.
Quote source: "abstract"

Outcome quote: In the replication of Study 1, we failed to find support for the positive correlation between the number of high level action identification and overall mind attribution (original: r = .3, 90% CI [.024, .53]; replication: r = .08, 90% CI [-.07, .24]). || In the replication of Study 2, we found support for the liking effect on action identification (original: -- 2 = .22, 90% CI [.024, .47]; replication: --2 = 0.28, 90% CI [.20, .36]) and mind attribution (original: --2 = .23, 90% CI [.031, .49]; replication: --2 = .19, 90% CI [.12, .26]). || In the replication of Study 3, we failed to find support for the suffering effect on action identification (original: --2 = .062, 90% CI [0, .22]; replication: --2 = .01, 90% CI [0, .05]) and mind attribution (original: --2 = 0.07, 90% CI [0, 0.23]; replication: --2 = .003, 90% CI [0, .02]). || In the replication of Study 5, we failed to find support for the perspective-taking effect on action identification (original: --2 = .094, 90% CI [0, .304]; replication: --2 = .002, 90% CI [0, .03]).
